mechanism_narrative: Positive payroll momentum raises expected household income and near-term revenue growth; higher 3-month PAYEMS growth therefore predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when evaluated as a continuous trend signal.
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS 3-month growth signal has Rank IC below 0.03 in more than half of rolling 3-year walk-forward windows or if positive-payroll-momentum months do not outperform negative-momentum months.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above its trailing 12-month average tightens discount-rate conditions and lowers equity duration valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its trailing 12-month moving average and the 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the triggered mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth above inflation-sensitive trend signals stronger nominal demand and near-term earnings support; rising RSAFS momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between RSAFS growth and CPIAUCSL growth is nonpositive in walk-forward regressions or if triggered 60-day returns are not higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production acceleration indicates improving operating leverage and broader cyclical demand for listed firms, so positive 3-month INDPRO momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum months have lower average 20-day forward returns than nonpositive momentum months in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and weaker expected consumption, so an upward UNRATE shock predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the triggered mean return exceeds the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts contraction reduces construction activity, durable-goods demand, and credit-sensitive spending, so falling HOUST momentum predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 6-month growth is negative while FEDFUNDS 6-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition has no incremental explanatory power over HOUST alone or if triggered 60-day returns are not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration raises the expected path of policy rates and compresses equity valuation multiples, so faster CPIAUCSL momentum predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above its trailing 12-month growth rate
failure_condition: Reject if CPI acceleration triggers are followed by nonnegative average 5-day S&P 500 returns or if adding FEDFUNDS does not improve out-of-sample directional accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Real output growth above labor-market slack signals a broad expansion with earnings support, so higher GDPC1 growth combined with low UNRATE predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth is positive and UNRATE is below 5.0
failure_condition: Reject if triggered low-unemployment growth expansions do not produce higher 60-day returns than nontriggered periods or if the interaction coefficient is not positive in walk-forward regressions.
